subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: The Promise of the Holy Spirit
context: Jesus' promise to send the Holy Spirit to His disciples as recorded in John 14 and 16:5-16, and Acts 1:3-8, explaining the role and importance of the Comforter

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: The Coming of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost
context: The fulfillment of Jesus' promise on the day of Pentecost with visible and audible signs including tongues of fire, mighty wind, and speaking in different languages as recorded in Acts 2:1-13

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Peter's First Speech and Its Effects
context: Peter's powerful sermon on the day of Pentecost explaining the events, calling for repentance, and the conversion of about 3,000 people as recorded in Acts 2:14-41

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: The togetherness, sharing, worship, and communal life of early believers as they devoted themselves to apostles' teaching, fellowship, breaking of bread, and prayer (Acts 2:41-47)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Dealing with Fraud and Dishonesty in the Church
context: The story of Ananias and Sapphira who lied to the Holy Spirit about their offering, demonstrating God's judgment against fraud and the importance of honesty (Acts 5:1-11)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Murmuring and the Selection of Deacons
context: How the early church resolved complaints about unfair distribution to widows by selecting seven deacons with good qualities to serve, ensuring equitable treatment (Acts 6:1-7)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Persecution of the Early Believers - Peter and John
context: The arrest of Peter and John by religious authorities for preaching about Jesus and performing miracles, and their bold testimony before the council (Acts 4:1-31)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Stephen's arrest, his powerful defense speech about the temple and the law, his vision of Jesus, and his death as the first Christian martyr (Acts 6:8-7:60)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Saul's (later Paul) aggressive persecution of Christians, including his role in Stephen's death and his efforts to destroy the church by imprisoning believers (Acts 8:1-3; 9:1ff)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: The persecution of the church under King Herod, including the execution of James the brother of John, and the arrest of Peter (Acts 12:1-3)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Peter's encounter with Simon the magician in Samaria who tried to buy the power of the Holy Spirit with money, and Peter's rebuke against simony (Acts 8:14-25)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Peter's Healing Miracles - Raising of Dorcas
context: The miraculous raising of Dorcas (Tabitha) from the dead by Peter in Joppa, demonstrating God's power and strengthening faith in the community (Acts 9:36-42)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Peter's vision about clean and unclean animals, his visit to Cornelius the centurion, and the significance of Gentiles receiving the Holy Spirit and being accepted into the church (Acts 10:1-48)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: How Peter was miraculously freed from prison by an angel while the church prayed for him, demonstrating God's power to deliver His servants (Acts 12:1-24)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Paul's Nativity, Education and Early Life
context: Paul's background as Saul of Tarsus, a Jew from the tribe of Benjamin, his education under Gamaliel, and his initial career as a Pharisee (Acts 22:1-5, 26-30)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: Paul's Conversion on the Road to Damascus
context: The dramatic conversion of Saul when he encountered Jesus on the way to Damascus, his temporary blindness, and Ananias' role in his healing and baptism (Acts 9:1-19)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Biblical teachings on how Christians should respond to persecution including praying for enemies (Matthew 5:43-48), enduring suffering (John 15:18-25), and wise relocation when necessary (Matthew 10:16-26)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
topic: The Beginning of the Church in Antioch
context: How the church was established in Antioch of Syria after persecution scattered believers, and how it became the base for missionary work (Acts 11:19-30)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: The sending forth of Barnabas and Paul as missionaries by the Holy Spirit, and their visits to Salamis, Paphos, Perga, and Antioch in Pisidia (Acts 13:1-3)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Continuation of the first missionary journey covering visits to Iconium, Lystra, and Derbe, including challenges faced and conversions made during the journey

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: The convening of the first church council in Jerusalem to resolve the conflict about whether Gentile converts needed to follow Jewish law, and the decision reached (Acts 15:1-35)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Learning from the Jerusalem Council about proper ways to resolve conflicts through dialogue and peaceful discussion, versus wrong methods like fighting, vengeance, and violence

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Paul's second missionary journey with Silas, visiting churches to strengthen them, the Macedonian call, and establishing churches in Philippi, Thessalonica, and Corinth

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Paul's third missionary journey focusing on strengthening existing churches, his extended ministry in Ephesus, and his determination to go to Jerusalem despite warnings

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Understanding Christian organizations like World Council of Churches (WCC), Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), and how they promote unity among different denominations

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Studying biblical principles that foster unity including faith and works (James 1:22-27), humility (Philippians 2:3-11), impartiality (James 2:1-13), and forgiveness (Galatians 6:1-2)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Understanding the importance of Christians helping one another in times of need, and the biblical basis for generous giving as taught in Philippians 4:14-19

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: How Christians should live as salt and light in their communities (Matthew 5:13-16), being good examples and witnesses to non-believers (1 Peter 2:9-18)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Learning how to respond to persecution with faith and courage, maintaining Christian witness even in difficult circumstances (Matthew 5:11-12; 1 Peter 3:13-17)

subject: Christian Religious Studies
context: Practical ways Christians from different denominations can work together in service, evangelism, and community development while maintaining their distinct identities
